\"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} \"\n                    f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                )\n\n                # Create stuck card with fence context\n                return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = await self._check_fence_for_bead(result.bead_id)\n                if fence_context:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Detected immediate fence for bead {result.bead_id} self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n tasks to complete with per-source timeout enforcement\n111\t        # We use asyncio.wait with ALL_COMPLETED to detect window close:\n112\t        # - All tasks run concurrently\n113\t        # - Each task is wrapped to handle its own timeout\n114\t        # - As tasks complete, we process them immediately\n115\t        # - Window closes when ALL tasks reach terminal state (success/timeout/error)\n116\t        sources = {}\n117\t        succeeded = []\n118\t        timed_out = []\n119\t        failed = []\n120\t        skipped = []\n121\t        caveats = []\n122\t\n123\t        # Wrap each task with its timeout and error handling\n124\t        # This allows true concurrent waiting with per-source timeouts\n125\t        async def execute_with_timeout(\n126\t            source: FetchSource,\n127\t            required: bool,\n128\t            timeout: int | float,\n129\t            task: asyncio.Task\n130\t        ) -> tuple[FetchSource, bool, SourceResult]:\n131\t            \"\"\"Execute a task with timeout, returning (source, required, result).\"\"\"\n132\t            try:\n133\t                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n134\t                return source, required, result\n135\t            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n136\t                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n137\t                result = SourceResult(\n138\t                    source=source,\n139\t                    status=\"timeout\",\n140\t                    data={},\n141\t                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n142\t                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n143\t                )\n144\t                return source, required, result\n145\t            except Exception as e:\n146\t                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n147\t                result = SourceResult(\n148\t                    source=source,\n149\t                    status=\"error\",\n150\t                    data={},\n151\t                    error=str(e),\n152\t                    duration_ms=0,\n153\t                )\n154\t                return source, required, result\n155\t\n156\t        # Create wrapped tasks for concurrent execution\n157\t        wrapped_tasks = [\n158\t            execute_with_timeout(source, required, timeout, task)\n159\t            for source, required, timeout, task in tasks\n160\t        ]\n161\t\n162\t        # Wait for all wrapped tasks to complete concurrently\n163\t        # This is the WINDOW CLOSE detection: we wait until ALL tasks finish\n164\t        completed_results = await asyncio.gather(*wrapped_tasks)\n165\t\n166\t        # Process results as they arrive (maintaining completion order)\n167\t        for source, required, result in completed_results:\n168\t            # Stream partial result immediately (progressive window fill)\n169\t            if on_partial_result:\n170\t                on_partial_result(source, result)\n171\t\n172\t            sources[source] = result\n173\t\n174\t            if result.status == \"success\":\n175\t                succeeded.append(source)\n176\t                # Check if the source data contains a caveat (e.g., fallback path with narrower scope)\n177\t                if isinstance(result.data, dict) and \"caveat\" in result.data:\n178\t                    caveats.append(result.data[\"caveat\"])\n179\t            elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n180\t                timed_out.append(source)\n181\t                caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n182\t            elif result.status == \"error\":\n183\t                failed.append(source)\n184\t                if required:\n185\t                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n186\t                else:\n187\t                    caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n188\t\n189\t        # Build coverage report\n190\t        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n191\t            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n192\t            succeeded=succeeded,\n193\t            timed_out=timed_out,\n194\t            failed=failed,\n195\t            skipped=skipped,\n196\t        )\n197\t\n198\t        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n199\t\n200\t        result = FetchResult(\n201\t            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n202\t            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n203\t            sources=sources,\n204\t            coverage=coverage,\n205\t            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n206\t            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n207\t        )\n208\t\n209\t        logger.info(\n210\t            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n211\t            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n212\t            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n213\t        )\n214\t\n215\t        # Detect if ALL sources failed - this is a terminal failure condition\n216\t        # The caller (intent router) should broadcast all_sources_failed event\n217\t        if len(succeeded) == 0 and coverage.total_sources > 0:\n218\t            logger.error(\n219\t                f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n220\t                f\"{len(failed)} failed, {len(timed_out)} timed out\"\n221\t            )\n222\t            # Mark result with terminal failure flag\n223\t            result.terminal_failure = \"all_sources_failed\"\n224\t\n225\t        return result\n226\t\n227\t    async def _execute_source(\n228\t        self,\n229\t        spec,\n230\t        context: FetchContext,\n231\t    ) -> SourceResult:\n232\t        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n233\t        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n234\t        if not executor:\n235\t            return SourceResult(\n236\t                source=spec.source,\n237\t                status=\"error\",\n238\t                data={},\n239\t                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n240\t                duration_ms=0,\n241\t            )\n242\t\n243\t        start = time.time()\n244\t        try:\n245\t            data = await executor(context)\n246\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n247\t\n248\t            return SourceResult(\n249\t                source=spec.source,\n250\t                status=\"success\",\n251\t                data=data,\n252\t                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n253\t            )\n254\t\n255\t        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n256\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n257\t            return SourceResult(\n258\t                source=spec.source,\n259\t                status=\"timeout\",\n260\t                data={},\n261\t                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n262\t                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n263\t            )\n264\t\n265\t        except Exception as e:\n266\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n267\t            return SourceResult(\n268\t                source=spec.source,\n269\t                status=\"error\",\n270\t                data={},\n271\t                error=str(e),\n272\t                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n273\t            )\n274\t\n275\t    # Source executors\n276\t\n277\t    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n278\t        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n279\t resolved from the project's ``cluster`` via\n406\t        config/clusters.yaml (see src/fetch/clusters.py): there is no single\n407\t        ArgoCD API, and querying the wrong instance returns not-found —\n408\t        indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\". An unmapped cluster, or one\n409\t        mapped to an ``access`` mode the strand cannot satisfy (it holds no\n410\t        ArgoCD credentials, so only ``read-only-proxy`` is consumable), raises\n411\t        ``ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable`` *before* any HTTP call.\n412\t\n413\t        The exception is raised outside the httpx try/except so the resolution\n414\t        ``reason`` propagates cleanly: ``_execute_source`` buckets it as a\n415\t        failed source and the fetch loop emits a ``fetch_coverage`` caveat\n416\t        carrying the reason. Never a silent wrong-instance query.\n417\t        \"\"\"\n418\t        app_name = context.app_name or context.project_slug\n419\t        if not app_name:\n420\t            return {\"error\": \"No application name specified\"}\n421\t\n422\t        resolution = resolve_argocd_endpoint(context.cluster)\n423\t        if not resolution.satisfiable:\n424\t            raise resolution.argocd_api\n430\t        try:\n431\t            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0, verify=False) as client:\n432\t                resp = await client.get(\n433\t                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/applications\",\n434\t                    params={\"name\": app_name}\n435\t                )\n436\t                resp.raise_for_status()\n437\t                apps_data =    \"name\": app_name,\n448\t                    \"sync_status\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n449\t                    \"health_status\": status.get(\"health\", {}),\n452\t                    \"created_at\": status.get(\"operationState\", {}).get(\"startedAt\", \"\"),\n453\t                }\n454\t\n455\t        except Exception as e:\n456\t            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n457\t\n458\t    def _make_cmd(self, context: FetchContext, *args: str) -> list[str]:\n459\t        \"\"\"Wrap a command with SSH if the repo is remote.\"\"\"\n460\t        if context.repo_path\n468\t        if not repo_path:\n469\t            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n470\t\n471\t        if not context.ssh_target and not              commits.append({\n494\t                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n495\t                            \"message\": parts[1],\n496\t                            \"author\": )\n613\t\n614\t                stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n615\t\n616\t                if proc.returncode == 0:\n617\t                    import json as _json\n618\t orchestrator: coordinates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n\nRuns all fetch sources concurrently, provides incremental results via callbacks,\ntracks coverage, and surfaces caveats for failed sources.\n\nThis is the canonical fetch implementation, consolidating the command matrix,\nconcurrent execution, and coverage tracking into a single module.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport time\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, AsyncIterator, Callable, Optional\n\nimport httpx\n\nfrom get_fetch_commands,\n    get_required_sources,\n    SourceResult,\n)\nfrom .clusters import ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable, resolve_argocd_endpoint\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# Type alias for the streaming callback\n# Called when a source completes: (source, result) -> None\nStreamCallback = Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]\n\n\nclass FetchStrand:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch strand: concurrent data fetcher with streaming support.\n\n    Executes all fetch commands for an intent concurrently, streams partial\n    results as they arrive, and provides comprehensive coverage tracking.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._source_executors = {\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS: self._fetch_kubectl_deployments,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_WORKFLOWS: self._fetch_kubectl_workflows,\n            FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP: self._fetch_argocd_app,\n            FetchSource.GIT_LOG: self._fetch_git_log,\n            FetchSource.GIT_STATUS: self._fetch_git_status,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_LIST: self._fetch_bead_list,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_DETAILS: self._fetch_bead_details,\n            FetchSource.CI_STATUS: self._fetch_ci_status,\n            FetchSource.COMPONENTS: self._fetch_components,\n            FetchSource.LOGS: self._fetch_logs,\n            FetchSource.EVENTS: self._fetch_events,\n            FetchSource.SESSION_STATE: self._fetch_session_state,\n            FetchSource.TOPIC_CONTEXT: self._fetch_topic_context,\n            FetchSource.REMINDERS: self._fetch_reminders,\n            FetchSource.FS_EXPLORE: self._fetch_fs_explore,\n            FetchSource.FS_README: self._fetch_fs_readme,\n            FetchSource.FS_HOME: self._fetch_fs_home,\n        }\n\n    async def fetch(\n        self,\n        request: FetchRequest,\n        on_partial_result: Optional[Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]] = None,\n    ) -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request to execute\n            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with coverage information\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n        tasks = []\n        for spec in command_specs:\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n            )\n            # Get effective timeout from config file or spec default\n            effective_timeout = get_effective_timeout(spec, request.context.project_slug)\n            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, effective_timeout, task))\n\n        # Wait for all tasks to complete with per-source timeout enforcement\n        # We use asyncio.wait with ALL_COMPLETED to detect window close:\n        # - All tasks run concurrently\n        # - Each task is wrapped to handle its own timeout\n        # - As tasks complete, we process them immediately\n        # - Window closes when ALL tasks reach terminal state (success/timeout/error)\n        sources = {}\n        succeeded = []\n        timed_out = []\n        failed = []\n        skipped = []\n        caveats = []\n\n        # Wrap each task with its timeout and error handling\n        # This allows true concurrent waiting with per-source timeouts\n        async def execute_with_timeout(\n            source: FetchSource,\n            required: bool,\n            timeout: int | float,\n            task: asyncio.Task\n        ) -> tuple[FetchSource, bool, SourceResult]:\n            \"\"\"Execute a task with timeout, returning (source, required, result).\"\"\"\n            try:\n                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n                return source, required, result\n            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n                result = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"timeout\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n                )\n                return source, required, result\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                result = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"error\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=str(e),\n                    duration_ms=0,\n                )\n                return source, required, result\n\n        # Create wrapped tasks for concurrent execution\n        wrapped_tasks = [\n            execute_with_timeout(source, required, timeout, task)\n            for source, required, timeout, task in tasks\n        ]\n\n        # Wait for all wrapped tasks to complete concurrently\n        # This is the WINDOW CLOSE detection: we wait until ALL tasks finish\n        completed_results = await asyncio.gather(*wrapped_tasks)\n\n        # Process results as they arrive (maintaining completion order)\n        for source, required, result in completed_results:\n            # Stream partial result immediately (progressive window fill)\n            if on_partial_result:\n                on_partial_result(source, result)\n\n            sources[source] = result\n\n            if result.status == \"success\":\n                succeeded.append(source)\n                # Check if the source data contains a caveat (e.g., fallback path with narrower scope)\n                if isinstance(result.data, dict) and \"caveat\" in result.data:\n                    caveats.append(result.data[\"caveat\"])\n            elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n                timed_out.append(source)\n                caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n            elif result.status == \"error\":\n                failed.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n                else:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n\n        # Build coverage report\n        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n            succeeded=succeeded,\n            timed_out=timed_out,\n            failed=failed,\n            skipped=skipped,\n        )\n\n        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n\n        result = FetchResult(\n            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            sources=sources,\n            coverage=coverage,\n            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n        )\n\n        # Detect if ALL sources failed - this is a terminal failure condition\n        # The caller (intent router) should broadcast all_sources_failed event\n        if len(succeeded) == 0 and coverage.total_sources > 0:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n                f\"{len(failed)} failed, {len(timed_out)} timed out\"\n            )\n            # Mark result with terminal failure flag\n            result.terminal_failure = \"all_sources_failed\"\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_source(\n        self,\n        spec,\n        context: FetchContext,\n    ) -> SourceResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n        if not executor:\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n                duration_ms=0,\n            )\n\n        start = time.time()\n        try:\n            data = await executor(context)\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"success\",\n                data=data,\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"timeout\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=str(e),\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n    # Source executors\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n\n        if not namespace:\n            # Try to derive from project_slug\n            if context.project_slug:\n                namespace = context.project_slug.replace(\"-\", \"\")\n            else:\n                return {\"error\": \"No namespace specified\"}\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods\"\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                pods_data = resp.json()\n\n                pods = []\n                for pod in pods_data.get(\"items\", []):\n                    pod_name = pod.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"name\", \"\")\n                    phase = pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"phase\", \"Unknown\")\n                    ready = self._get_pod_ready(pod)\n                    restarts = sum(\n                        c.get(\"restartCount\", 0)\n                        for c in pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"containerStatuses\", [])\n                    )\n\n                    pods.append({\n                        \"name\": pod_name,\n                        \"phase\": phase,\n                        \"ready\": ready,\n                        \"restarts\": restarts,\n                    })\n\n                return {\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"pods\": pods,\n                    \"pod_count\": len(pods),\n                    \"healthy_count\": sum(1 for p in pods if p[\"phase\"] == \"Running\"),\n                }\n\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n                return {\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"error\": \"Namespace not found\",\n                    \"pods\": [],\n                }\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"pods\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_deployments(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch deployment status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n        deployment = context.deployment or context.app_name\n\n        if not namespace or not resp.json()\n\n                status = deploy_data.get(\"status\", {})\n                spec = deploy_data.get(\"spec\", {})\n\n                return {\n                    \"name\": deployment,\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"replicas\": spec.get(\"replicas\", 0),\n                    \"ready_replicas\": status.get(\"readyReplicas\", 0),\n                    \"available_replicas\": status.get(\"availableReplicas\", 0),\n                    \"updated_replicas\": status.get(\"updatedReplicas\", 0),\n                    \"conditions\": status.get(\"conditions\", []),\n                }\n\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n                return {\"error\": f\"Deployment not found: {deployment}\"}\n            raise\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_workflows(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch Argo Workflow status from CI cluster.\"\"\"\n        proxy = KUBECTL_PROXIES.get(\"iad-ci\", context.proxy)\n        project_slug = context.project_slug\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/namespaces/argo-workflows/workflows\",\n                    params={\"labelSelector\": f\"project={project_slug}\"} if project_slug else None\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                workflows_data = resp.json()\n\n                workflows = []\n                for wf in workflows_data.get(\"items\", []):\n                    workflows.append({\n                        \"name\": wf.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"name\", \"\"),\n                        \"phase\": workflows.sort(key=lambda x: x.get(\"started_at\", \"\"), reverse=True)\n\n                return {\n                    \"project\": project_slug,\n                    \"workflows\": _fetch_argocd_app(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch ArgoCD application status.\n\n        The ArgoCD endpoint is resolved from the project's ``cluster`` via\n        config/clusters.yaml (see src/fetch/clusters.py): there is no single\n        ArgoCD API, and querying the wrong instance returns not-found —\n        indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\". An unmapped cluster, or one\n        mapped to an ``access`` mode the strand cannot satisfy (it holds no\n        ArgoCD credentials, so only ``read-only-proxy`` is consumable), raises\n        ``ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable`` *before* any HTTP call.\n\n        The exception is raised outside the httpx try/except so the resolution\n        ``reason`` propagates cleanly: ``_execute_source`` buckets it as a\n        failed source and the fetch loop emits a ``fetch_coverage`` caveat\n        carrying the reason. Never a silent wrong-instance query.\n        \"\"\"\n        app_name = context.app_name or context.project_slug\n        if not app_name:\n            return {\"error\": \"No application name specified\"}\n\n        resolution = resolve_argocd_endpoint(context.cluster)\n        if not resolution.satisfiable:\n            raise ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable(\n                resolution.reason or \"ArgoCD endpoint unresolvable\",\n                cluster=context.cluster,\n            )\n\n        proxy = resolution.argocd_api\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0, verify=False) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/applications\",\n                    params={\"name\": app_name}\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                apps_data = resp.json()\n\n                if not apps_data.get(\"items\"):\n                    return {\"error\": f\"Application not found: {app_name}\"}\n\n                app = apps_data[\"items\"][0]\n                status = app.get(\"status\", {})\n                operation = app.get(\"operation\", {})\n\n                return {\n                    \"name\": app_name,\n                    \"sync_status\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"health_status\": status.get(\"health\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"revision\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"revision\", \"\"),\n                    \"operation\": operation.get(\"operation\", {}),\n                    \"created_at\": status.get(\"operationState\", {}).get(\"startedAt\", \"\"),\n                }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n    def _make_cmd(self, context: FetchContext, *args: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Wrap a command with SSH if the repo is remote.\"\"\"\n        if context.ssh_target:\n            return [\"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target, \" \".join(args)]\n        return list(args)\n\n    async def _fetch_git_log(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git log for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Fetch commits with author and date\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"log\", \"-10\",\n                                \"--pretty=format:%h|%s|%an|%ar\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            commits = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line:\n                    parts = line.split(\"|\", 3)\n                    if len(parts) == 4:\n                        commits.append({\n                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n                            \"message\": parts[1],\n                            \"author\": parts[2],\n                            \"date\": parts[3],\n                        })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"branch\", \"--show-current\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"commits\": commits,\n                \"count\": len(commits),\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"commits\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_git_status(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git status for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Get status output\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"status\", \"--short\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            # Parse status output - format: XY filename\n            changed_files = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line and len(line) >= 3:\n                    status_code = line[:2]\n                    filename = line[3:]\n                    changed_files.append({\n                        \"status\": status_code,\n                        \"file\": filename,\n                    })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"branch\", \"--show-current\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            # Get last commit\n            log_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"log\", \"-1\", \"--oneline\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            log_stdout, _ = await log_proc.communicate()\n            last_commit = log_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"last_commit\": last_commit,\n                \"changed_files\": changed_files,\n                \"count\": len(changed_files),\n                \"has_changes\": len(changed_files) > 0,\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"changed_files\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_bead_list(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch bead list from the appropriate workspace.\n\n        Per plan Beads-Workspace Scoping:\n        - Primary path: run `bf list --status open` (NO --project filter) inside the\n          project's repo_path checkout when it has a .beads/ workspace\n        - Fallback (no repo_path/.beads): list from the adc workspace filtered\n          --project {slug}, with a fetch_coverage caveat naming the narrower scope\n        \"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        project_slug = context.project_slug\n\n        # ADC workspace for fallback path (all aide-de-camp-originated beads live here)\n        adc_workspace = \"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"\n\n        try:\n            if context.ssh_target:\n                # Remote: check for .beads and run bf via SSH in one shot\n                proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                    \"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target,\n                    f\"cd {repo_path} && [ -f .beads/issues.jsonl ] && bf list --status=open --limit=50 2>&1 || echo '{{\\\"error\\\":\\\"no beads workspace\\\"}}'\",\n                    stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                )\n\n                stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n                if proc.returncode == 0:\n                    import json as _json\n                    try:\n                        beads = _json.loads(stdout.decode())\n                    except Exception:\n                        beads = []\n                    return {\n                        \"project\": project_slug,\n                        \"repo\": context.display_path if hasattr(context, \"display_path\") else repo_path,\n                        \"host\": context.host_alias,\n                        \"beads\": beads,\n                        \"count\": len(beads) if isinstance(beads, list) else 0,\n                        \"scope\": \"project_workspace\",  # Primary path\n                    }\n                else:\n                    error_output = stderr.decode().strip() or stdout.decode().strip()\n                    if \"no beads workspace\" in error_output or \"No such file\" in error_output:\n                        # Fallback path: no local .beads workspace at remote\n                        # Use adc workspace with --project filter\n                        return await self._fetch_bead_list_from_adc(project_slug, repo_path, context, remote_ssh=context.ssh_target)\n                    return {\"error\": error_output or \"bf list returned non-zero\"}\n            else:\n                # Local: check for .beads workspace first\n                from pathlib import Path as _Path\n\n                if repo_path and (_Path(repo_path) / \".beads\" / \"issues.jsonl\").exists():\n                    # Primary path: project has its own .beads/ workspace\n                    # Run bf list without --project filter (a project's own workspace\n                    # doesn't tag its beads with the aide-de-camp slug)\n                    proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                        \"bf\", \"list\", \"--status=open\", \"--limit=50\",\n                        stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                        cwd=repo_path,\n                    )\n\n                    stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n                    if proc.returncode == 0:\n                        import json as _json\n                        try:\n                            beads = _json.loads(stdout.decode())\n                        except Exception:\n                            beads = []\n                        return {\n                            \"project\": project_slug,\n                            \"repo\": repo_path,\n                            \"beads\": beads,\n                            \"count\": len(beads) if isinstance(beads, list) else 0,\n                            \"scope\": \"project_workspace\",  # Primary path\n                        }\n                    else:\n                        return {\"error\": stderr.decode().strip() or \"bf list returned non-zero\"}\n                else:\n                    # Fallback path: no local .beads workspace or no repo_path\n                    # Use adc workspace with --project filter\n                    return await self._fetch_bead_list_from_adc(project_slug, repo_path, context)\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return {\"error\": \"bf CLI not found in PATH\"}\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n    async def _fetch_bead_list_from_adc(\n        self, project_slug: str | None, repo_path: str | None, context: FetchContext, remote_ssh: str | None = None\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch bead list from adc workspace with --project filter (fallback path).\n\n        Per plan Beads-Workspace Scoping fallback:\n        \"showing aide-de-camp-originated beads only\" with caveat about narrower scope.\n\n        Returns beads filtered by --project {slug} from adc workspace.\n        \"\"\"\n        adc_workspace = \"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"\n\n        if not project_slug:\n            return {\"error\": \"No project_slug for fallback bead list\"}\n\n        try:\n            if remote_ssh:\n                # Remote fallback: run bf list from adc workspace via SSH\n                proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                    \"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    remote_ssh,\n                    f\"cd {adc_workspace} && bf list --project={project_slug} --status=open --limit=50\",\n                    stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                )\n            else:\n                # Local fallback: run bf list from adc workspace\n                proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                    \"bf\", \"list\", f\"--project={project_slug}\", \"--status=open\", \"--limit=50\",\n                    stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    cwd=adc_workspace,\n                )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode == 0:\n                import json as _json\n                try:\n                    beads = _json.loads(stdout.decode())\n                except Exception:\n                    beads = []\n                return {\n                    \"project\": project_slug,\n                    \"repo\": repo_path,\n                    \"beads\": beads,\n                    \"count\": len(beads) if isinstance(beads, list) else 0,\n                    \"scope\": \"adc_workspace_filtered\",  # Fallback path\n                    \"caveat\": f\"No local beads workspace for {project_slug}; showing aide-de-camp-originated beads only\",\n                }\n            else:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode().strip() or \"bf list returned non-zero\"}\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return {\"error\": \"bf CLI not found in PATH\"}\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n resolved repo directory (local or remote).\"\"\"\n        if not context.repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved — cannot explore filesystem\"}\n\n        if context.ssh_target:\n            try:\n                proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                    \"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target,\n                    f\"ls -la {context.repo_path} 2>&1\",\n                    stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                )\n                stdout, _ = await proc.communicate()\n                return {\n                    \"path\": f\"{context.ssh_target}:{context.repo_path}\",\n                    \"host\": context.host_alias,\n                    \"listing\": stdout.decode(),\n                }\n            except Exception as e:\n                return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n        from pathlib import Path as _Path\n        explore_path = _Path(context.repo_path)\n        if not explore_path.exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Path not found: {explore_path}\"}\n\n        items = []\n        try:\n            for entry in sorted(explore_path.iterdir()):\n                if entry.name.startswith(\".\"):\n                    continue\n                items.append({\n                    \"name\": entry.name,\n                    \"type\": \"dir\" if entry.is_dir() else \"file\",\n                    \"size\": entry.stat().st_size if entry.is_file() else None,\n                })\n        except PermissionError as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n        return {\"path\": str(explore_path), \"items\": items, \"count\": len(items)}\n\n    async def _fetch_fs_readme(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Read the README from the resolved repo directory (local or remote).\"\"\"\n        if not context.repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n\n        if context.ssh_target:\n            try:\n                script = \" || \".join(\n                    f\"cat {context.repo_path}/{name} 2>/dev/null\"\n                    for name in (\"README.md\", \"README.txt\", \"README.rst\", \"README\")\n                )\n                proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                    \"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target, script,\n                    stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n
